highlights.title: PyroDash: Cost-Efficient Token-Level Small-Large Language Model Collaborative Inference
highlights.summary: PyroDash is a cost-aware framework for token-level SLM-LLM collaborative inference.
highlights.detail: PyroDash trains the SLM in three stages and balances answer accuracy against inference cost. It requires neither a separate router nor LLM retraining. The framework is useful for applications where both speed and accuracy are crucial.

highlights.title: Small, Free, and Effective: Orchestrating Open-Weight Small Language Models to Outperform Single LLM for Malware Analysis
highlights.summary: Orchestrated ensembles of small language models can match single LLM performance on malware analysis.
highlights.detail: The study investigates the effectiveness of small language models in malware analysis, which is essential for cybersecurity. The results show that ensembles of small models can be a viable alternative to large language models.

highlights.title: LKValues: Aligning Large Language Models with Sri Lankan Societal Values
highlights.summary: LKValues is a resource suite for Sri Lankan value alignment in large language models.
highlights.detail: LKValues addresses the cultural bias in large language models by providing a survey-grounded resource suite for Sri Lankan value alignment. This is crucial for developing culturally sensitive AI models.

highlights.title: Upcoming: Codeforces Round (Div. 2)
highlights.detail: The contest will start on August 1 and will last for 2 hours.

highlights.title: Qoreo: Choreographic Programming for Quantum Distributed Systems
highlights.detail: Qoreo is a choreographic programming language for quantum distributed systems. It includes a local quantum language with linear types and a choreographic language that combines local quantum computation with inter-actor classical and quantum communication.

items.title: PyroDash: Cost-Efficient Token-Level Small-Large Language Model Collaborative Inference
items.summary: PyroDash is a cost-aware framework for token-level SLM-LLM collaborative inference.
items.detail: PyroDash trains the SLM in three stages and balances answer accuracy against inference cost. It requires neither a separate router nor LLM retraining. The framework is useful for applications where both speed and accuracy are crucial.

items.title: Small, Free, and Effective: Orchestrating Open-Weight Small Language Models to Outperform Single LLM for Malware Analysis
items.summary: Orchestrated ensembles of small language models can match single LLM performance on malware analysis.
items.detail: The study investigates the effectiveness of small language models in malware analysis, which is essential for cybersecurity. The results show that ensembles of small models can be a viable alternative to large language models.

items.title: LKValues: Aligning Large Language Models with Sri Lankan Societal Values
items.summary: LKValues is a resource suite for Sri Lankan value alignment in large language models.
items.detail: LKValues addresses the cultural bias in large language models by providing a survey-grounded resource suite for Sri Lankan value alignment. This is crucial for developing culturally sensitive AI models.

items.title: GigaToken: ~1000x faster Language model tokenization
items.summary: GigaToken is a language model tokenization system.
items.detail: GigaToken aims to improve the efficiency of language model tokenization, which is a critical component of natural language processing. Faster tokenization can lead to better performance and reduced latency.

items.title: Introducing OpenAI Presence
items.summary: OpenAI Presence is an enterprise AI agent platform.
items.detail: OpenAI Presence is designed to help organizations deploy trusted AI agents for customer and internal workflows. The platform aims to provide a reliable and efficient way to integrate AI into business operations.

items.title: Accelerating the frontiers of scientific discovery: Google’s $40M commitment to the Genesis Mission
items.summary: Google commits $40M to the Genesis Mission.
items.detail: The commitment is part of Google's effort to accelerate scientific discovery using AI. The Genesis Mission aims to advance our understanding of the world through cutting-edge research and innovation.

items.title: Advancing the next era of national science
items.summary: OpenAI outlines its commitment to advancing American science.
items.detail: OpenAI's commitment is focused on using frontier AI to accelerate discovery and drive innovation in the scientific community. The partnership with the U.S. Department of Energy and national labs aims to leverage AI for the betterment of society.

items.title: Building AI infrastructure with the Effingham County community
items.summary: OpenAI announces Project Camellia in Effingham County, Georgia.
items.detail: Project Camellia is part of OpenAI's effort to promote responsible energy, community investment, and job creation. The project aims to provide access to Codex and other AI resources to the local community.

items.title: Jul 21, 2026 Announcements Anthropic is donating another $20 million to Public First Action
items.summary: Anthropic donates $20 million to Public First Action.
items.detail: The donation is part of Anthropic's commitment to supporting organizations that align with its values and mission. Public First Action is a non-profit organization focused on promoting public interest and social welfare.

items.title: Jul 22, 2026 Economic Research A research agenda for the Economic Futures Research Fund
items.summary: Anthropic outlines a research agenda for the Economic Futures Research Fund.
items.detail: The research agenda is focused on exploring the potential applications and implications of AI in the economic sector. The goal is to provide insights and recommendations for policymakers and stakeholders.

items.title: Security updates for Wednesday
items.summary: Security updates have been issued by multiple Linux distributions.
items.detail: The security updates address various vulnerabilities and issues in different Linux distributions. Users are advised to apply the updates to ensure the security and stability of their systems.

items.title: [$] Attaching programs to multiple tracepoints
items.summary: BPF programs can now be attached to multiple tracepoints.
items.detail: The update allows for more flexible and efficient use of BPF programs in kernel tracing and debugging. This is a significant improvement for developers and system administrators.

items.title: [$] LWN.net Weekly Edition for July 23, 2026
items.summary: LWN.net Weekly Edition for July 23, 2026 released
items.detail: The edition covers various topics including LLMs in the kernel, GNOME save and restore, and BPF and tracepoints. It also includes briefs on GNOME security, PyPI policy, and Firefox 153.

items.title: PyPI now rejects new files after 14 days
items.summary: PyPI rejects new files after 14 days
items.detail: The restriction is to prevent the poisoning of old releases if publishing tokens or workflows of PyPI projects are compromised. This change was made after the popular packages LiteLLM and Telnyx were compromised.

items.title: [$] Save and restore may be coming to GNOME
items.summary: Save and restore may come to GNOME
items.detail: The feature is expected to provide a platform-wide save and restore framework for GNOME. It has been attempted twice before but is expected to succeed this time.

items.title: An LLM in the Kitchen
items.summary: LLM in the kitchen
items.detail: The article discusses the use of LLMs in recipe search and how it can help with finding specific details in a recipe.

items.title: Upcoming: Codeforces Round (Div. 2)
items.summary: Codeforces Round starts August 1
items.detail: The contest will start on August 1 and will last for 2 hours.

items.title: Qoreo: Choreographic Programming for Quantum Distributed Systems
items.summary: Qoreo programming language introduced
items.detail: Qoreo is a choreographic programming language for quantum distributed systems. It includes a local quantum language with linear types and a choreographic language that combines local quantum computation with inter-actor classical and quantum communication.

items.title: Hollywood: Towards a Large Movie Dataset for Database Benchmarking
items.summary: Hollywood movie dataset introduced
items.detail: Hollywood is a synthetic IMDb-compatible benchmark generator that combines LLM-generated semantic dictionaries with deterministic temporal-graph-based relational data generation. It contains 200,000 primary movies and 19.7M IMDb-style rows.

items.title: Linearising Explicit Substitutions using Intersection Types
items.summary: Linearising explicit substitutions using intersection types
items.detail: The paper defines a new term expansion for a calculus with explicit substitutions, using it to relate a lambda-calculus with explicit substitutions to Boudol's resource aware lambda-calculus with multiplicities.

items.title: Odin: Primitive-Level Synchronization for Distributed Point-Based Neural Rendering
items.summary: Odin synchronization system introduced
items.detail: Odin is a distributed point-based neural rendering training system that replaces global barriers with primitive-level synchronization. It uses a ahead-of-time scheduler and runtime validation to identify low-conflict overlap windows.

items.title: RCC: Speculative Write Versioning with Redo Logs
items.summary: RCC speculative write versioning introduced
items.detail: RCC is a system that leverages redo logs to resolve conflicting writes. It allows concurrent transactions to be pipelined after the update-conflicting point, enabling lightweight rollback.

items.title: TriAgent: Divergence-Aware Multi-Agent Committees for Cost-Efficient Financial Sentiment Analysis
items.summary: TriAgent multi-agent committee introduced
items.detail: TriAgent is a multi-agent committee stratified by contextual granularity for cost-efficient financial sentiment analysis. It uses a three-way Semantic Divergence Index to route queries accordingly.

items.title: LLMQuant/quant-mind
items.summary: QuantMind framework introduced
items.detail: QuantMind is an agent-native knowledge extraction and retrieval framework for quantitative finance.

items.title: NVIDIA/Model-Optimizer
items.summary: NVIDIA's Model-Optimizer library optimizes deep learning models
items.detail: It supports various downstream deployment frameworks and compresses models for faster inference speed. The library includes techniques like quantization and neural architecture search.

items.title: ZSeven-W/openpencil
items.summary: Open-source AI-native vector design tool OpenPencil released
items.detail: It features concurrent Agent Teams and allows designing UI directly on the live canvas. OpenPencil is a modern alternative to Pencil.

items.title: assimp/assimp
items.summary: Assimp library loads 40+ 3D-file-formats
items.detail: The library provides a unified and clean data structure for loaded 3D models. It is useful for various applications that require 3D model loading.

items.title: catchorg/Catch2
items.summary: Catch2 is a modern C++ test framework
items.detail: It supports unit-tests, TDD, and BDD. Catch2 also has support for older C++ versions in separate branches.

items.title: gabime/spdlog
items.summary: Spdlog is a fast C++ logging library
items.detail: It is designed to be highly performant and efficient. Spdlog can be used in various applications that require fast logging capabilities.
